title: 15 Years Later, Beloved Shonen Jump Character Returns to The Main Story
content: Katsura Hoshino’s beloved D.Gray-man manga debuted in the Weekly Shonen Jump magazine in 2004 but eventually faced numerous hiatuses due to her poor health. In 2018, the manga moved to a quarterly schedule in Jump SQ. Rise , leading to a far slower trickle of new chapters.
author: ComicBook

title: 'Welcome to Demon School, Iruma-kun' Season 4 Casts Miki Nagasawa, Yuka Terasaki
content: The staff for the anime of Osamu Nishi 's Welcome to Demon School, Iruma-kun ( Mairimashita! Iruma-kun ) manga confirmed two cast members for the music festival judges in the anime's fourth season on Saturday. Both characters appeared in episode 5 on Saturday.
author: ANN

title: Black Clover: Every Romance Confirmed in Finale, Ranked By How Surprising They Are
content: Black Clover has officially ended its run after 11 long years, and with its final chapter has confirmed some very surprising couples. Black Clover has wrapped up its run with Shueisha’s various magazine with the final three chapters of its story hitting earlier this week, and with them revealed the look at how Asta…
author: ComicBook

title: 5 Years Later, Major Crunchyroll Anime Confirms Sequel Return
content: Five years after it ended its original TV anime return, one major anime now streaming with Crunchyroll has confirmed it’s coming back with a new TV sequel anime. Fans keeping a close eye on things might have noticed how some of the biggest anime franchises have been able to make their comebacks with all kinds of new…
author: ComicBook

title: Jujutsu Kaisen Creator Confirms the Story is Over: “It Won’t Continue!”
content: Jujutsu Kaisen ‘s creator has confirmed that the long running story has come to an end after the release of its final sequel’s volume. Gege Akutami originally wrapped up Jujutsu Kaisen ‘s run to an end with Shueisha’s Weekly Shonen Jump magazine in 2024, but made a surprising return last year with the debut of a full…
author: ComicBook

title: 2 Years Later, Major Shonen Jump Series Confirms It Saved Itself From Cancellation
content: It turns out that one major series running in Shueisha’s Weekly Shonen Jump magazine was able to save itself from cancellation, and has gone on for two years since. Shonen Jump has been going through a lot of changes in the past few years as following the loss of some of the magazine’s longest running hits in quick…
author: ComicBook

title: Black Clover Creator Breaks Silence on Ending With Final Goodbye
content: Black Clover has officially come to an end after 11 years , and the creator behind it all has shared a final goodbye message to fans. Black Clover has been working through the final arc of its run ever since it had shifted over to new quarterly release schedule with Shueisha’s Jump GIGA magazine back in 2023.
author: ComicBook

title: Crunchyroll Is Finally Streaming One of 2025’s Most Requested Movies
content: One of the coolest movies from last year is now streaming with Crunchyroll as fans have been eagerly waiting for its streaming release for a while. Last year was stacked with some of the most commercially and critically successful anime film releases in quite a long time, and fans have been waiting for many of these…
author: ComicBook

title: Daemons of the Shadow Realm Anime Casts Akira Ishida
content: The staff for the television anime of Hiromu Arakawa 's Daemons of the Shadow Realm ( Yomi no Tsugai ) manga confirmed on Sunday that Akira Ishida is playing Asuma Kagemori. Asuma is the second-born son of the Kagemori family, and he appeared in episode 5 on Saturday.
author: ANN

title: KidariStudio Wraps Filming for Short-Form Live-Action Adaptation of Wild Eyes Webtoon
content: Korean webtoon and web novel production company Kidari Studio revealed on April 28 it is moving forward with its latest webtoon adaptation for the newly launched short-form video platform Lezhin Snack with a live-action adaptation of the Wild Eyes webtoon.
author: ANN
